items.title: Govt Maintains Subsidised Prices Amid Global Oil Market Volatility
items.summary: MOF kept BUDI95 at RM1.99, held the 200-litre limit, and maintained regional diesel support despite recent global volatility.

items.title: MOF: Pump Fuel Prices Reflect Last Week’s Costs Despite Global Drop
items.summary: The ministry said Malaysia’s pump prices lag the current headline market because the weekly average cost is what drives the calculation.

items.title: Claims Of Additional BUDI95 Subsidy Application Untrue, 200-litre Monthly Limit Remains - MOF
items.summary: MOF said the public cannot freely apply for higher BUDI95 limits and that the temporary 200-litre cap remains in force.

items.title: RON97 Retail Price Down 20 Sen, Diesel Up 50 Sen - MOF
items.summary: MOF confirmed a weekly retail update that lowered RON97 to RM4.95, lifted Peninsular diesel to RM6.02, and kept Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan diesel at RM2.15 for 2 to 8 April.

items.title: Amid regional conflict, the Strait of Hormuz remains critical oil chokepoint
items.summary: EIA says the strait moved about 20 million barrels per day in 2024, roughly one-fifth of global petroleum liquids consumption.
